resposta:
```python
class Solution:
    def minimumOneBitOperations(self, n: int) -> int:
        return n ^ (n - 1) if n else 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses bitwise operations to find the minimum number of operations to transform n into 0. The expression n ^ (n - 1) works by flipping all bits to the right of the rightmost 1 in n, effectively counting the number of operations needed to transform n into 0.
